item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Fragile Cease-Fire Takes Hold as Both Sides Claim Victory
item.description: Global relief at the pause in fighting was tempered by uncertainty over what happens next. Israel declared its support for the two-week truce between the U.S. and Iran, but said it did not include its war against Hezbollah in Lebanon.

item.title: Even as They Praise Iran Cease-Fire, World Leaders Are Whipsawed by Trump
item.description: Across Europe and the globe, the war has damaged economies, roiled politics and underscored a lack of options in dealing with President Trump’s whims.

item.title: ‘There are no winners’: US and Iran enter into fragile truce
item.description: Fragile truce does not bring permanent end to war between foes burdened by decades of distrust

item.title: War Decimated Iran’s Leadership and Pushed Up a New Generation
item.description: Since the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, authority has shifted to the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps. Younger military leaders have come to the fore.

item.title: Madagascar declares state of emergency over severe fuel shortages linked to Iran war
item.description: The presidency says it is taking action as it fears fuel shortages could lead to public disorder.

item.title: Ian Cheshire named new Ofcom chair
item.description: Ex-Kingfisher boss, and former Channel 4 chair, will replace Lord Michael Grade at the media regulator

item.title: Iran Cease-Fire Does Little to Convince Netanyahu’s Critics in Israel
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had already declared victory over Iran once, in June last year. Few Israelis believe that the goals he set this time will be met, either.

item.title: Exxon warns of $6.5bn hit from Iran war as hedging timing masks gains
item.description: US oil group says bulk of impact on first-quarter earnings is accounting issue that will be offset as contracts complete

item.title: Extreme weather is already costing global businesses
item.description: Rio Tinto’s $800mn revenue hit highlights growing costs of business interruption

item.title: The new super-prime dilemma: sell, rent or manage?
item.description: For those who have moved their primary residence away from London, a sluggish sales market is prompting them to weigh up their options

item.title: Starmer to visit Middle East after US and Iran reach ceasefire deal
item.description: The prime minister welcomes the deal, saying the UK will do "all we can to support and sustain this ceasefire".

item.title: US and Iran agree 2-week ceasefire that will open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Donald Trump says 10-point plan from Iran is ‘workable basis’ for negotiations

item.title: Hospitals coping well with doctors' strike so far - NHS boss
item.description: Resident doctors in England – the new name for junior doctors – are taking part in their 15th walkout in a long-running pay dispute.

item.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon after US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: Air strikes hit the Tyre and Nabatieh areas hours after the US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

item.title: Delta Expects Strong Profit Despite Higher Fuel Costs
item.description: The airline said demand for tickets remained very strong despite the effects of the war in Iran.

item.title: How I became a meme in Nigeria
item.description: My moment of internet fame reflects the country’s sceptical political mood

item.title: China Pressed Iran Toward Cease-fire, Iranian Officials Say
item.description: Beijing appeared to have helped push Tehran to accept the two-week deal with the United States, reflecting China’s growing influence and its stake in avoiding a wider war.

item.title: Delta to cut routes and raise charges to counter $2bn jet fuel hit
item.description: US airline faces higher bill from soaring prices driven by Iran war

item.title: Gulf Nations Face New Reality, Whether Cease-Fire Holds or Not
item.description: The countries will have to re-evaluate their relationships with Israel, Iran and the United States after a war that has exposed their vulnerability.

item.title: European repo is absolutely massive
item.description: Repurchasing power parity

item.title: Submit your questions: How will the Iran war impact China?
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with Joe Leahy and Cheng Leng from our Beijing bureau on Friday April 10 at 1pm BST

item.title: Hedge funds suffered worst losses since Covid in Iran war turmoil
item.description: Managers have struggled to navigate huge market swings sparked by conflict

item.title: Five Belgian minimalist designers to know
item.description: Meet the interiors experts upholding a very clean tradition

item.title: Toyota keeps hydrogen as a hedge
item.description: Retaining flexibility may prove more valuable than going all in too early on a single bet

item.title: FirstFT: Trump and Iran announce 2-week ceasefire
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Perplexity’s revenues jump 50% in a month and Turkey sells $20bn of gold

item.title: British teen dies in reported Vietnam motorbike fall
item.description: Orla Wates is remembered by her mother as "beautiful, independent and very funny".

item.title: Armageddon is off . . . for now
item.description: Both the US and Iran can claim victory of a sort but many questions remain unresolved

item.title: European bonds surge as traders trim bets on interest rate rises
item.description: Government debt in UK and Eurozone on course for strongest day since 2023

item.title: Israel Says Iran Cease-Fire Does Not Extend to Lebanon
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu contradicted an earlier statement from Pakistan that said the deal included Lebanon, where Israel is at war with the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.

item.title: UK house prices fall in March as uncertainty over Middle East war weighs on demand
item.description: Halifax reports market slowdown as mortgage rates rise

item.title: Iran conflict has deepened UK political instability
item.description: Pressure on living standards as a result of hostilities will damage Labour

item.title: Oil prices plunge and shares jump on US-Iran ceasefire plan
item.description: Crude prices tumbled by as much as 15% on the conditional pause but remain far higher than before the war.

item.title: Shell’s oil traders get earnings boost from Iran war
item.description: FTSE 100 group expects a drop in gas production because of hit to facilities in Qatar

item.title: Why You Can’t ‘Flick a Switch’ to Get Oil and Gas Flowing Out of the Persian Gulf
item.description: Some wells can be turned on in days or weeks, but bringing the Gulf’s energy system back to something akin to normal will take months.

item.title: Aldermore Bank put up for sale after owner attacks UK car finance redress scheme
item.description: South Africa’s FirstRand says industry-wide £9.1bn programme is ‘deeply flawed’

item.title: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
item.description: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

item.title: Iran and America agree to pause their war
item.description: A two-week truce is a start but questions remain about a more enduring deal

item.title: He Got Rich Buying and Selling Luxury Watches. Was It a Ponzi Scheme?
item.description: Dominic Khoo made waves in Singapore as an investor in expensive timepieces. Now many of his clients accuse him of fraud.

item.title: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
item.description: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

item.title: Top Australian soldier charged with war crimes to remain in jail on remand
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith faces five charges of the war crime of murder, which has a penalty of life in prison.

item.title: Shelly Kittleson, American Journalist Abducted in Iraq, Is Freed
item.description: The journalist, Shelly Kittleson, was abducted by a militia allied with Iran and held for a week. Iraqi officials say she was freed in exchange for the release of militia members.

item.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson released after kidnap in Iraq, officials say
item.description: US officials say they are now "working to support her safe departure from Iraq".

item.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
item.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

item.title: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
item.description: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

item.title: ‘I Love Viktor’: Trump and Vance Cheer on Orban in Hungarian Race
item.description: The American leaders slathered on the praise for the nationalist standard-bearer just days before an election he could lose.

item.title: Honduran newlywed wife of US soldier freed by ICE after detention
item.description: Annie Ramos, an undocumented immigrant who came to the US as a toddler, had gone to the base to complete paperwork so the couple could move in together.

item.title: Trump condemned over threat that Iran's 'civilisation will die'
item.description: The UN's chief says he is "deeply troubled" by such statements, as the US president ramps up pressure on Iran to agree a deal to end the war.

item.title: The crew of Artemis II are returning to a planet they have cheered up
item.description: The mission has recaptured some of the enthusiasm of early lunar flights

item.title: Kanye West ‘Should Never Have Been Invited,’ Starmer Says After Barring Rapper From UK
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s government blocked the artist formerly known as Kanye West from entering Britain for the three-day Wireless Festival in July.

item.title: Wireless Festival cancelled after Kanye West blocked from coming to UK
item.description: West was due to headline the festival in July but drew criticism over past antisemitic comments.

item.title: French couple leave Iran after more than three years in jail
item.description: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris are on their way home after being allowed to leave the country, the French president says.

item.title: JD Vance backs Orbán's re-election bid in Budapest visit and hits out at EU
item.description: US Vice-President JD Vance intervenes in the campaign to give Orbán a ringing endorsement in Budapest.

item.title: Fifa starts disciplinary action over Spain v Egypt chants
item.description: Fifa opens disciplinary proceedings against the Spanish FA after "Islamophobic and xenophobic" chants during a friendly between Spain and Egypt last month.

item.title: Did Israel overestimate the damage to Iran’s missile programme?
item.description: Iran shows no sign of running out

item.title: Schools in knife crime hotspots to get specialist training
item.description: A new partnership will provide training for school leaders about knife-crime risk, the government says.

item.title: Middle East Dispatch newsletter: A terrifying wait
item.description: Gregg Carlstrom, our Middle East correspondent, on Donald Trump’s blood-curdling threat to erase Iran

item.title: A wary rapprochement between India and China
item.description: New Indian investment rules are a cautious start

item.title: Pakistan Says It Hit a Military Target. Investigations Suggest It Was a Rehab Center.
item.description: After the deadliest attack in Afghanistan since the Taliban took over, families searched among photos and remains for signs of their relatives.

item.title: Scottish Conservatives pledge tax cuts in election manifesto
item.description: Leader Russell Findlay announces his party's plans ahead of next month's Holyrood poll.

item.title: Bahamas police search for US woman who reportedly fell off boat
item.description: Her husband told authorities she fell out of their dinghy on Saturday night and was swept out to sea.

item.title: One gunman killed and two injured in shooting at Israeli consulate in Istanbul
item.description: No Israeli diplomats are currently in Turkey and the Istanbul consulate has been empty for the past two-and-a-half years.

item.title: Kim Jong-un’s Daughter Drives a Tank as Talk Accelerates Around North Korea Succession
item.description: Images of Kim Ju-ae at the helm of the military vehicle, with her father riding on top, added to speculation that she was being groomed to succeed him as North Korea’s leader.

item.title: A Hidden Russian Hand in Hungary’s Election? Actually, It’s Quite Open.
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has made hostility to Ukraine a centerpiece of his campaign. Moscow seems determined to repay the favor.

item.title: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage
item.description: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.

item.title: WHO suspends Gaza medical evacuations after contractor killed by Israeli troops
item.description: Israel's military says troops fired at a vehicle driven by the Palestinian because they believed it posed "an immediate threat".

item.title: Ringleader of suspected human trafficking network arrested in Ethiopia
item.description: Yetbarek Dawit is alleged to have operated a multi-million-dollar operation, using torture to raise money.

item.title: Dozens killed as Angola flood death toll rises
item.description: Mourning the deaths, the president says it was a "race against the clock" to find others affected.

item.title: Reform would deny visas over calls for slavery reparations
item.description: The party says it wants to put African and Caribbean countries "on notice" after a vote at the UN last month.

item.title: One dead as train travelling 99mph collides with lorry in France
item.description: The lorry driver is in custody after the train driver died during the collision in northern France.

item.title: Greens promise rent controls and £1 bus fares in Welsh election manifesto
item.description: The Greens' set of promises for the Senedd election also include free bus travel for under-22s.

item.title: Chilean woman accused of Pinochet-era kidnaps loses extradition battle
item.description: Adriana Rivas moved from Chile to Australia in the 1970s, working there as a nanny and cleaner.

item.title: Plan 2 student loan interest rates capped at 6% in England
item.description: The cap on Plan 2 and postgraduate loan interest rates comes amid a risk of rising inflation.

item.title: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
item.description: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.

item.title: More than 1,700 Brits who fell ill in Cape Verde join action against Tui
item.description: Tui is investigating the claims and says it is "not in a position to provide a statement at this stage".

item.title: Air India CEO steps down early as losses mount
item.description: Wilson, whose term was set to end in 2027, will remain CEO and MD until a successor is appointed.

item.title: Australia Charges Ex-Soldier With War Crimes in Afghanistan
item.description: The suspect, charged with five counts of murder, is accused of killing or ordering the killings of unarmed civilians during the Afghan war.

item.title: Ukraine Ramps Up Attacks on Russian Oil, Aiming to Curb Iran War Windfall
item.description: As the Persian Gulf conflict boosts the oil revenue that finances Moscow’s war against Ukraine, Kyiv’s forces are striking at Russia’s ability to refine and ship its crude.

item.title: Australia's most-decorated living soldier charged over alleged war crimes
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith, who denies all wrongdoing, previously lost a landmark defamation case over the alleged murders.

item.title: Why Hungary’s Election Could Swing on Roma Votes
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s policies affecting the Roma minority have put those voters in play in upcoming parliamentary elections. In a tight race, they could make the difference.

item.title: India's top court hears challenges to ruling on women's entry into temple
item.description: Experts say the court's decision will have far-reaching consequences for women's religious freedoms in India.

item.title: Minnesota deny Colombia's Rodriguez has rare muscle wasting condition
item.description: Minnesota United deny rumours that midfielder James Rodriguez is suffering from a potentially life-threatening muscle-wasting condition.

item.title: Why Real Madrid tie could make or break Kane's Ballon d'Or hopes
item.description: Harry Kane is the leading goalscorer in Europe but does he need to win a major trophy to have a chance of winning the prestigious award?

item.title: In Paris’s Catacombs, Can a Restoration Breathe New Life Into City’s Dead?
item.description: For centuries, the bones of some six million people were buried in the catacombs beneath the city. Curators are trying to preserve and modernize the tunnels while maintaining the spooky ambience.

item.title: Trump's deadline looms but Asian nations already have deals with Iran
item.description: Nations in the region have been keen to reach agreements as their economies are heavily reliant on Middle East energy.

item.title: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound
item.description: Almost nothing was known about the Royal Hill casino until the Thai military took control of it in December.

item.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
item.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

item.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
item.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

item.title: Nine policemen sentenced to death in India over Covid custody killings
item.description: The father and son died in jail after being arrested on suspicion of breaching Covid lockdown rules in 2020.

item.title: Iran and America are spinning stories about the size of their victories
item.description: The bombast obscures the scale of the damage the war is doing

item.title: Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
item.description: The Israeli strikes reportedly targeted Hamas security personnel after they clashed with an Israeli-backed militia near Maghazi camp.

item.title: Seven Eritrean players fail to return home after international match
item.description: Several footballers have absconded in the past leaving behind what rights groups call a repressive government.

item.title: Ladysmith Black Mambazo's 'wise elder' dies aged 77
item.description: Albert Mazibuko sang in the iconic South African choral group for more than 55 years.

item.title: 'We're sinking deeper': Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
item.description: Ordinary Iranians respond to the US president's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: The strange, multicultural slang of Toronto’s teenagers
item.description: London rappers have an outsize influence

item.title: Taiwan and China are preparing for a summit, of sorts
item.description: A trip to the mainland by the KMT’s leader exposes a rift in the party

item.title: How far do the European Union’s military ambitions go?
item.description: An interview with the EU’s top military officer

item.title: Pogacar may face fine for running red light in Flanders win
item.description: Tadej Pogacar wins a record-equalling third Tour of Flanders, but could face action after he is one of up to 20 riders who run a red light at a railway crossing.

item.title: Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh
item.description: More than 100 people, mostly children, have been killed by measles since mid-March, officials suspect.

item.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
item.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

item.title: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
item.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

item.title: 'I adore her now': Mother learns to live with child's autism in a country with little help
item.description: Malawian Martha Ongwane, brought low by her daughter's autism, found a rare support group.

item.title: Spain's huge pork industry seeks salvation from swine fever threat
item.description: Countries around the world, including the US, have already stopped imports over the outbreak.

item.title: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing's ambition
item.description: The AI agent sparked a frenzy of "raising lobsters" in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.

item.title: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
item.description: It comes a week before votes are cast and follows warnings of so-called false flag operations blamed on Ukraine.

item.title: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?
item.description: Since 2010, Orbán has transformed Hungary into what the European Parliament has denounced as a "hybrid regime of electoral autocracy".

item.title: The South American petro-state profiting from the Iran war
item.description: The boom is raising the risk that oil money devours the economy

item.title: Pope Leo calls for global leaders to choose peace in his first Easter Mass
item.description: Pope Leo XIV addressed thousands of worshippers gathered in St Peter's Square on Easter Sunday in his first address as pontiff.

item.title: European allies are losing hope of keeping America in NATO
item.description: Donald Trump is furious at their refusal to help his war against Iran
